meta.description: What's your stress response type? Fight, Flight, Freeze, or Fawn? Take this 8-question quiz to discover how you react under pressure!
meta.keywords: stress response test, fight flight freeze fawn, trauma response, stress type quiz, psychology test
meta.og_description: 8 questions reveal your stress response type!
meta.twitter_description: 8 questions reveal your stress response type!

question.0: When someone criticizes you unfairly, you...
question.0a: Fight back and defend yourself immediately
question.0b: Leave the situation as fast as possible
question.0c: Freeze up and can't find words to respond
question.0d: Agree with them to keep the peace
question.1: Your boss assigns an impossible deadline, you...
question.1a: Argue that it's unreasonable and push back
question.1b: Secretly start job searching that evening
question.1c: Stare blankly at the task, unable to start
question.1d: Say 'Sure, no problem!' with a smile
question.2: A friend cancels plans last minute, you...
question.2b: Immediately make other plans to stay busy
question.2c: Sit alone, unsure what to do with yourself
question.2d: Say 'Don't worry about it!' and reassure them
question.3a: Double down and refuse to back off
question.3c: Go completely silent and shut down
question.3d: Apologize even when you know you're right
question.4b: Escape to social media or distractions
question.4c: Stare at the list, completely paralyzed
question.4d: Ask everyone else what they need first
question.5: Someone cuts you off in traffic, you...
question.5a: Honk, yell, and let them know about it
question.5b: Take a different route to avoid them
question.5c: Grip the steering wheel, frozen in shock
question.5d: Wave them ahead and let them go
question.6: You receive harsh feedback on your work, you...
question.6a: Defend your choices and push back
question.6b: Start looking for other projects immediately
question.6c: Reread it numbly, unable to process
question.6d: Rewrite everything to please them completely
question.7: When facing a major life decision, you...
question.7a: Decide quickly and commit with full force
question.7b: Avoid thinking about it as long as possible
question.7d: Ask everyone else what they think you should do

result.percentile: Only {pct} of all participants are the {type} type

fighter.name: Fighter
fighter.tagline: Confrontational, takes control, channels stress into action
fighter.description: When stress hits, you don't back down — you charge forward. Your nervous system defaults to confrontation, turning anxiety into aggression and fear into fire. You argue, you push back, you take control. While this can be incredibly empowering and protective, it can also escalate conflicts unnecessarily. You'd rather burn bridges than feel powerless. Your strength is your courage, but your challenge is knowing when to stand down.
fighter.trait3: Would rather fight than feel helpless

flighter.name: Flighter
flighter.tagline: Escapes, avoids, stays busy to outrun the stress
flighter.description: Your survival instinct is to run — not always physically, but emotionally. When stress shows up, you escape. You stay busy, overwork, scroll endlessly, or simply vanish from situations that feel threatening. Avoidance is your armor. You're the master of distraction, keeping yourself so occupied that you never have to sit with uncomfortable feelings. Your strength is adaptability, but avoidance means problems keep following you.

freezer.name: Freezer
freezer.tagline: Shuts down, dissociates, goes numb under pressure
freezer.description: When the world gets too loud, you go quiet. Stress doesn't make you fight or flee — it makes you freeze. You shut down, dissociate, zone out. Decisions feel impossible. Your brain hits pause, and you become a spectator in your own life. This isn't laziness or apathy — it's your nervous system going into protective shutdown. Your challenge is learning to thaw, to reconnect, to move when everything in you says stay still.

fawner.name: Fawner
fawner.tagline: People-pleases, over-accommodates, loses self to keep peace
fawner.description: Your stress response is to become whatever others need you to be. You people-please, over-accommodate, agree when you disagree, and smile when you're hurting. Conflict terrifies you — not because you're weak, but because your survival strategy is to be non-threatening. You've learned that making others happy keeps you safe. Your gift is deep empathy, but the cost is losing yourself entirely. You matter too.

share.text: My stress response type is '{type}'! What's yours?
